How to Create
Irresistible Sales
Messages for ANY
Business
Module 1: Cracking the Copy Code
With Dan Kennedy and John Carlton
www.gkic.com
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 4
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Cracking the Copy Code
You know the slogan, “Give a man a fish and feed him for a day; teach a
man to fish and you feed him for a lifetime.”
The part of that which is not often quoted is “Teach a man to sell fish and you
feed him for a lifetime without the cursed monotony of eating only fish!”
So copywriting is really salesmanship in print.
Good copywriting is good selling, that's what it is.
In USA Today, there is a full page ad for some contest they're running where you
get to do an ad for USA Today: “Give us your creative. Win $1 million in
advertising.”
So you get to do an ad for USA Today but the first problem is you've got judges
that don't know anything about how to sell anything, who are now going to pass
judgment on whatever it is you do.
But it gets worse. Here is their statement about
the competition: “Print advertising is an art and
you’re the artist.”
They couldn’t be more wrong.
Art is art. We are not sure what that is. Some
people claim to know it when they see it; at the
National Endowment of the Arts, we give money for pictures of people peeing
on crosses and we call that art.
We have a wide range of what we consider to be art but anybody who thinks
advertising is art is a moron.
The point of
advertising is not to
have somebody like
it so much they wish
to put it in a frame.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 5
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
The point of advertising is not to have somebody like it so much they wish to put
it in a frame and put it up on their wall. That’s not the point of advertising.
The point of advertising is to sell something.
The judges in that competition undoubtedly do not think so. They will judge it
on criteria that has nothing to do with whether or not it is capable of selling
anything.
Most of you live in the real world which is where advertising is often bought for
a dollar with a hot check and must produce revenue before the check clears.
That’s the real world. It’s definitely the world I grew up in:
 Buy postage stamps with hot check
 Drop stuff in mail
 Hope money arrives before check clears
There’s an old trick you used to be able to do that you can’t do now. You used to
be able to take an X-Acto knife and cut through the numbers on the bottom of
the check. That bought you an extra four or five days.
I bet I didn’t issue a check for three years that I didn’t pull out a razor blade and
put a little thing through it. You talk about hope as a strategy, we were running
on that.
When that’s the world you live in, it ain’t art – unless it’s about a guy named Art.
It ain’t art; it’s about selling stuff.
When you get that copy is selling and selling is really copy, then
you’re a long way towards being able to either produce for
yourself, or know when you see it produced for you, effective copy.
There’s very simple tests, if you stood up on stage and read it aloud, would
people run to the back of the room and buy things?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 6
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Simple test, when you read it aloud to yourself, is this what you would say to
somebody if you were face to face with them across the kitchen table or across
the conference table trying to sell them something.
If it’s not, it’s probably not good copy.
The Secret Code
There is actually a secret code to successful info-marketing copy in just about
every category there is.
There’s a million little ones but basically the categories that the info-marketing
business really deals with are:
 Health – in there we find fitness, beauty, weight loss
 Finance – where we find the investment stuff, the personal finance stuff;
the how to make money in stocks, how to make money in commodities,
how to make money in real estate, how to make money in llamas and the
doom and gloom, Armageddon, the end of the world crowd
 Opportunity
 Niche business improvement
 Personal growth and self-improvement
 Hobbies
That pretty much is the list of the main categories in info-marketing.
There is a secret copy code behind every one of those categories. Whichever one
or ones apply to you, I’m going to tell how to go crack the code for yourself and
how to reverse the code into a template from which you can rather efficiently
create, for the most part, serviceable copy.
Now, it may not be John Carlton level copy; it may not be Dan Kennedy level
copy but it will be serviceable copy. For most of your purposes in most of your
places, you will be able to get good and effective results working from this
process:
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 7
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
 Crack the code
 Convert the code
 Do a template
 Write to fit the template
That’s what we’re going to do.
Why We Need a Code
Here’s why it’s necessary. The reason we want to steal other people’s copy
codes is because… now if the word “steal” disturbs you, there’s ethical theft and
there’s unethical theft.
In this case, unethical theft is theft of copy verbatim. For that, you should go to
jail and you actually can go to jail if somebody is very aggressive about it.
I’m talking about stealing the architecture. I’m talking about stealing the
framework on which you now hang the words. I consider that ethical theft.
The reason why it’s necessary to steal other people’s copy codes is because, in
most cases, most of you can’t do enough testing to genuinely get to success by
your own testing.
The big guys in any category do mammoth amounts of tests. They do big
horizontal tests – 20, 30 different headlines.
The illustration below is just the beginning of a test matrix. This is why you have
to steal code.
If we are going to test four lists and
we’re going to test 5,000 names on each
of the four lists, and we are going to do
direct mail, we’ll just start with this:
 We have headline A, headline B
 Now we have offer A which we
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 8
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
have to test with headline A but we also have to test offer A with headline
B
 Then we have price A which we have to test with headline A with offer A
but then we have price A we have to test with headline A with offer B etc.,
etc.
Just this far, which is not a complete test, at 5,000 units a cell, I’m up to 400,000
pieces in the mail in order to run the test.
Most of you are in niches that don’t even have 400,000 customers in the first
place.
If we try to build our own code from scratch through our own testing, either:
 We won’t live that long – some of us are closer to the end than others but
I’m telling you, nobody in here would live that long
 We’ll get weary
 We’ll go broke
One of those bad things is going to happen long before we crack the codes
through our own devices.
So we want to steal codes from somebody who is
able to do a lot of testing with the people we
want to sell to – preferably with something
comparable to what it is that we want to sell.
The good news for you is that, once you’ve
identified who they are, they can’t hide their
code.
 They put it in the mail
 They put it in print advertising
 They put it up on the web
 They send it out in email which you can sign up to get
Once you’ve
identified who they
are, they can’t hide
their code.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 9
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
They can’t hide it.
Now, it’s buried – but it can be unearthed from what it is that they do.
The example I’m going to show, I deliberately picked a category that very few
people in the room, if any, are in so that nobody has a bias about their own
experience in this category.
But understand, whatever category or categories are most relevant – are closest
to you – you want to go do to them what I’m going to show you I did to this one.
Cracking the Code
This is the code behind the investment newsletter part of our industry. They are
privately held companies for the most part, so it’s hard to get a grip on it, but it
is at least a $1 billion a year industry. It may be closer to a $10 billion a year
industry. Nobody is really sure but it’s big.
That world is the Armageddon guys who publish newsletters about the end of
the economy as we know it and what you’re supposed to do to cope with it.
If you’re unfamiliar with it, all you’ve got to do is find one, subscribe to one, and
you will soon be getting all of the mail from all of them because everybody of
course rents and shares everybody else’s lists in that category.
They are all information marketers. Almost all their businesses revolve around a
newsletter, some of them revolve around books and courses. Many of them then
have ascension models that are not very different from what you’re used to
seeing with us.
They put people into seminars and conferences. They put people into different
levels of membership. They often have five or six brands under one roof that
they are running.
So the investment newsletter category, here’s their code.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 10
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Now, I want you to understand how I got their code and I’m going to show you
some examples. I did what you will do. I took all their stuff and I “Napoleon
Hilled” it.
I hunted for the commonalities. I stick counted the commonalities out of six
bazillion pieces of their direct mail and I created a code, not in any particular
order, by the way, we’ll get to that.
Their code is as follows. Again these are not in priority order, they’re just
numbered to make it easier to reference them.
One is alarm. They all used the psychological trigger of alarm. There is some
impending disaster just around the corner; the black helicopters are coming. All
of your stuff is going to be confiscated; there’ll be no food; there’ll be no water.
There’ll be no colloidal silver, there’ll be no whatever and you’ve got to get some
right now.
Lee Bellinger, who’s very big in this business, one of their back-end products – I
think it’s $599 – is a pre-packaged get-out-of-your-house-in-90-minutes kit. It’s
got an axe and a box of Band-Aids and some dried food and a flashlight all put
together in a box. That’s one of their back-end products.
They live on alarm. You will not find hardly any pitch, no matter how positive it
is otherwise, where they have not used alarm somewhere in it in this entire
industry.
Second, mystery and mystique; this industry is very big on the secrets THEY
don’t want you to know.
The only difference is really who they are but there is a “they” who does not
want you to know things.
Three, amazing stories of people like the reader. Whoever it’s aimed at, there’s
going to be stories of people just like them.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 11
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
People they can identify with; people who appear to be – by description –
ordinary, normal, everyday folk who are using this super XYZ formula to make
money even as the economy crashes around them.
Four, authority-figure expert and media verification – somebody verifies what it
is that they’re saying.
Five, open acknowledgement that what they are saying is not to be believed,
that it’s strange, that it’s weird, that it’s “Gee, if this is true, how come you’re the
first person I’m hearing it from? That doesn’t make sense to me.”
They openly acknowledge that.
In almost every piece of copy you will find in this industry this piece exists.
Sixth, they answer the question of why “this” is different because it must be
different.
This must be different than everything that has gone before because this is not
the first piece of mail, this is not the first webinar, this is not the first email
you’re getting in this category, if
you’re a buyer in this category.
You’re getting a lot of it – like
every day – so it must be
different.
Your enemy is my enemy, thus
I’m on your side; you will find
that in almost every case.
If it’s good enough for
somebody famous then it’s good
enough for you.
The Copy Code: Investment Newsletters
1. Alarm
2. Mystery / Mystique
3. Amazing Stories of People Like Them
4. Authority-Figure, Expert And Media
Verification
5. Acknowledgement of "Incredible"
6. Why This Is Different
7. Your Enemy Is My Enemy
8. If It’s Good Enough For (Insert Famous
Name) It’s Good Enough For You
9. Pronouncements of Epic And Profound
Importance
10. Emotional Mission Statement
11. Crisis = Opportunity
12. Exotic, Unusual, Proprietary Name for
Your Solution
13. Tastes Like Chicken
14. "What To Do" (Not "How To")
15. Timeliness / Urgency
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 12
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Pronouncements of epic and profound importance, you won’t find these people
being subtle or talking about small things.
They will talk about big, “this changes everything” sort of things.
Ten, some sort of emotional mission statement – a reason why somebody is
there, is showing up now to tell you the story, to offer you this proposition.
Pretty much without a mission statement story or an origin story, it doesn’t
make sense.
Batman is a fine example. Many people know the Batman origin story. It’s been
told over and over and over again to make sure everybody gets it because
otherwise he don’t make any sense. Otherwise, he’s a freak in a costume dressed
up like a bat running around and beating the crap out of everybody.
This is not a heroic thing. This is a scary thing. This is not good without the
origin story, so there’s an origin story.
Crisis to opportunity and/or privilege; so, yes there’s a crisis – that’s the alarm –
but, if you know certain things, if you’re in the right place, if you’re connected to
the right person, it really means opportunity.
Twelve, some exotic, unusual, proprietary named solution. We have to rename
everything in this field.
However, when you do the exotic, weird, strange, you’ve never heard it told this
way before, you then have to explain it as, “It tastes like chicken.”
In all of this copy, you will find, “You’ve never heard of this before but, rest easy,
it tastes just like chicken.” That’s an important copy technique. That’s what
everybody tells you when they’re trying to get you to eat something you
shouldn’t eat.
In today’s USA Today or Wall Street Journal, they’ve got an article about some
restaurant somewhere that’s serving kangaroo 56 different ways and 80% of
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 13
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
the people are ordering kangaroo appetizers. I promise you, there’s four people
sitting at a table and three of them are going “kangaroo?” and the other person
is going, “It tastes just like chicken.”
To which my answer, by the way, is, “Let’s order chicken.”
Fourteen, the “what to do” without the “how to do it” and timeliness, recency
and urgency.
Example: Current Control
Here is a control right now in this category. There’s a pre-headline across the
top:
“Government Restricts Ads for…”
That’s number two on your
list – Mystery and Mystique.
“US 801(k) Plans.”
Many people know what a
401(k) plan is but how many
know what an 801(k) is?
Tastes just like chicken!
It sounds familiar enough,
“401(k), I’ve heard of that.
Okay, there’s probably an
801(k).”
They did a “man on the
street” the other night. I
forget which show I caught it
on but they went out and
asked people if today they
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 14
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
were all going to vote in the vice-presidential election and who they were going
to vote for.
To a person, they either swore they had just gone and done it – lying like dogs –
“Yeah, I went this morning and voted in the vice-presidential election” or they
said they were going to go vote in the vice-presidential election.
None of them went, there is no such thing as a vice-presidential election.
Nobody does that here either. Not a single person that gets this in the mail
immediately goes, “There’s no such thing as an 801(k) plan.”
That’s not how they react. They react, “401(k) I’ve heard of that. Maybe I should
have heard of an 801(k).”
That qualifies as number 12 and number 13 on your list.
“Why they could help make 401(k)s and IRAs obsolete.”
That qualifies as a number nine. That’s a pronouncement of epic importance.
It doesn’t say they’re better than 401(k)s. It doesn’t say they are more
financially rewarding than 401(k)s. It says, they will make 401(k)s and IRAs
obsolete. So that qualifies as a pronouncement of epic importance.
Here’s a paragraph:
“One national news journal says….”
Now there’s other references as we go along that are named but still that’s a
number four, verification.
The first sentence is:
“Don’t be surprised if you’ve never heard about….”
That’s the acknowledgement. That’s number five on your list, acknowledgement
that this is incredible.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 15
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
“Sound too good to be true? It’s not.”
That’s exactly how people react. Understand this is a highly-tested control from
a highly-sophisticated marketing company that is making huge sums of money
with this letter. They know what they’re doing.
Then we go in to a story, number three.
The story continues and there’s some other examples on the next page again of
ordinary folk:
 The Rousseau family of East Hartford, Connecticut got an 801(k) plan with
250 bucks and now it’s worth 19 grand.
 63-year-old Percy Schwartz of Clinton, New Jersey, his $166 is now $84,666.
 101-year-old Andrew Canter from New York City has turned a $1,842 stake
into eight million using the 801(k) plan strategy.
By the way, that’s not on your list, it should be, I missed one. So add on a
number 16, “meaningful specifics.”
It’s very important that he’s a 101 and he’s in New York and that Percy is 63 and
is in Camden and all that stuff is important.
Number four on your list, we have authority figures and the verifications:
 Robert Luke, who does financial research for the Atlanta Journal,
says…
 Market Watch says…
 Tom O’Hara of the National Association of Investors says…
 Kenneth Hooker, who writes for the Boston Globe says…
That’s number four on your code list.
Here’s a beauty:
“IRS worker turns $1,842 into $8.1 million.”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 16
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
It turns out the 101-year-old guy worked for the IRS. So who more credible to
tell us about 801(k) plans that the government doesn’t want you to know about
than a guy from the government who’s ratting them out? Perfect, he’s perfect.
Number nine, profound/epic:
“Retire Rich On Just $10 A Month.”
You should know, by the way, this is going to an Investor’s Business Daily
reader. This is not going to Roseanne Barr
in a trailer park somewhere.
Number six, why is this different? We’ve got
charts that show you why it’s different.
You’ll never decipher these charts; it don’t
matter. There’s charts, that’s proof, proof
that it’s different. Because, “Look it’s got
charts!”
Number six, how are we different?
“We are not brokers. We don’t manage money and, unlike Wall Street
brokers, our analysts never trade the securities they recommend.”
That makes them different. If you stop to think about it, it doesn’t make them
better, “We would never invest in the shit we’re telling you to buy…” but it does
make them different.
Eight on your list, “If it’s good enough for…”
“George Bush’s Secret Retirement Legacy.”
It turns out George Bush has got one of these plans, how about that?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 17
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Next, same code, different
letter:
“On December 21,
2012, America’s Most
Popular Investment
Will Reach Its Use-By
Date.”
It will be a civilization-
altering development.
That’s number nine on your code list.
Number four,
“A reviewer in Newsweek…. and the Wall Street Journal….”
Now what’s funny about this verification, this is what’s called negative
verification. This is, “Because they attack me, I must be real.”
“A reviewer in Newsweek dismissed this as an unthinking attack on
reason.”
“The Wall Street Journal categorically denounced it as no more than the
natterings of a dopey ant.”
If you’re worried about your negative reviews, this is what to do with them. You
use them as verification because, if you’re being attacked, you must know
something pretty cool.
By the way, that’s a lot easier to do
than continuing to push stuff further
down in the Google listings; just take
it off and use it when somebody calls
you a “nattering ant.”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 18
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Number eleven, crisis equals opportunity:
“But if you were on the right side, you could seize opportunities that only
come along once or twice in a century.”
Here’s our number 10 on our list – emotional mission statement:
“Civilization is about to confront its greatest crisis ever. It’s why I have been
impelled to return from retirement. I have been warning that this day
would arrive for many years now and I am returning to the public eye to
complete the job I began long ago. It will be the fulfillment of my life’s
work.”
Number eleven, crisis equals opportunity:
“In fact, I believe more money will be lost and made in the 18 months
following this event than ever before.”
Crisis = opportunity.
This is a different one now. This is a
current control. Number one alarm
and number nine pronouncement of
epic importance:
“The Mother of All Financial
Bubbles Is Just Now Starting
to Pop.”
Then we have our number 14 –
what to do not how to do it.
Here’s what we need to do…
Step 1: Make Sure You Live
(And Work!) Some Place Safe.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 19
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Then it tells us about safe places.
It doesn’t tell us where they are. It doesn’t tell us how we get there but it tells we
should be living in a safe place.
Step #2: Move Your Money Out Of Harm’s Way
Makes sense. How do we do that? It doesn’t tell us how to, it just tells us.
Step #3: Use Underground Banking
It doesn’t tell us how to do that. Some guy’s out in his backyard digging now
looking for the bank, he didn’t know he had it!
That is “what to” not “how to.”
Here’s another control, here’s number eight.
“…including the vice-president of Chevron and even the managers of a $125
million dollar pension plan in San Francisco.”
If it’s good enough for them, it’s good for….
So there’s your code list from this industry.
How to Crack Any Code
Now, if you take any category of
info-marketing, if you take niches,
and took all the marketing and lined
it up on a table and carefully went
through and identified something
that they all did and wrote it down,
you would crack their code.
Here’s how to crack any code.
How to Crack Any Code
At Least 12 – 24 Historical/Classic
Controls
At Least 6 – 12 Contemporary Controls
Identify Commonalities
Create Code Checklist
Create Swipe File for Each Commonality
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 20
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Number one, you need at least 12 to 24 historical controls. You don’t just want
contemporary because sometimes the current people drift away from what
works.
So you want a dozen to 24 historical controls.
If I enter a new category, whether it’s in info-marketing or in something else, I
go looking for the history of what was being sold in that category five years ago,
10 years ago, 20 years ago and I try and find some controls that have history.
I will at least go, say, to the trade journals.
I’m rewriting right now lead-generation ads in the chiropractic field for Dr.
Tomshack at HealthSource who’s a franchisor. I wrote his first lead-generation
ads, he’s been living on them for a few years. They’re starting to wane so I’ve
now got to write them again.
Even though I know a lot, I wanted every chiropractic trade journal for the past
two years so I could go through and I could find my controls and I could make
sure I had the code list right before I wrote the copy. So we want historical
controls.
Then we want six to 12 contemporary controls. We want to find the people who
are selling – preferably the bigger players because we know they’re doing a lot
of testing – to the buyers we’re going to be selling to and who are selling
something for which they are going to need to make the same kind of pitch we
would need to make.
Understand it doesn’t have to be exactly; so, in the opportunity category, if I sell,
“How to get rich in the carpet cleaning business,” I don’t just need carpet
cleaning; Ron Legrand’s stuff about how to get rich in real estate counts.
His controls count because the premise is how to get rich. The shovel we do it
with is really not particularly relevant when you write copy; you can plug that in
at the last minute.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 21
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
So I want historical controls and I want contemporary controls.
Then we sit down with them and we search for the commonalities.
For example, if every one of these has “tastes like chicken,” we write down
“tastes like chicken.”
We’re looking for the commonalities, that’s where
the code list comes from.
If this sounds arduous, by the way, I built this one for the financial newsletters
when we started to do a lot of work two years ago for the financial advisers and
I updated it again about three months ago for the
financial advisers.
Although they don’t sell newsletters, they don’t
sell books, they actually want clients for whom
they’re going to manage money or from whom
they’re going to take money.
However, they’re selling to the same client, the same person that these guys are
selling to and they’re basically selling the same premise, “You need to do
something different with your money than you’re doing now and you need to do
it quick.”
These guys are doing all kind of testing. My local adviser in Scottsdale, Arizona
can’t do that kind of testing so I’ve got to steal this code and use it for him.
So we’re going to identify commonalities.
Fourth, then, we create our code list just like I created a list. That’s going to
govern all of our copywriting.
If you want to get real sophisticated – if you want to do a little work so it’s faster
to do a lot of work – then you create a swipe file for each thing on your code list.
I have a swipe file for
every item that is on
my code list.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 22
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
I can now go back to a drawer and I can pull a file that just says “Alarm” at the
top and has all the alarm stuff copied and put in there.
Then I can go pull the “tastes like chicken” folder and, in there, is all the “tastes
like chicken” stuff.
I’ve cut it up. I’ve copied it and I put it in my file folders.
You could do this on your computer if you like. I just like paper.
I have a swipe file for every item that is on my code list.
Criteria for a Code Owner Worth Watching
Now, the criteria of a code owner worth watching – because you don’t want to
be random about this – number one, they’ve got to be doing direct response.
They can’t be doing brand or image stuff. They have to be doing real, honest-to-
God direct response.
Secondly, they could be doing online and they could be doing broadcast but it’s
better if they are doing print and direct mail too. They’re spending more money
doing print and direct mail than they are online. Therefore, they tend to invest
more in it.
In case you haven’t noticed, most of the email you get is not as
well-crafted as most of the mail you get.
That’s not because it’s necessarily less important. It’s just because people have
an attitude about it. It’s cheap to send out and we usually do it quick and at the
last minute.
Nobody wants to pay a copywriter $50,000 to write an email. They’re happy to
pay a copywriter $50,000 to write a print ad. So you want to study the print ads
more than you study the email.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 23
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Ideally, you want it to be a company of size so they can be doing a lot of testing.
They can be doing the testing you can’t.
Number four, obviously, you want them
active in your category or a comparable
category.
Number five, you want some longevity,
some evidence they’ve been running
multiple promotions over a period of
time.
You really don’t want to be following an amateur around who is learning day to
day just as you are.
How to Find the Code Owners
To find the code owners, look in the specialty interest publications that your
readers, that your customers, that your buyers read.
Find code owners’ ads, answer them – buy from them if you wish, if you must –
get all their stuff.
In a niche or subculture, if you are
not in the niche, you are not in the
subculture – let’s say you’re
someone in restaurant marketing
who never owned a restaurant, what
you need is a buddy who owns a
restaurant.
Because you need three months of
that guy’s junk mail.
If you don’t have a buddy, just go pay somebody. They put a big laundry basket
in their office or their house and they throw all their junk mail in it.
How to Find and Get Information About
Code Owners
Trade or Specialty Interest Publications
3 Months of a Prospect’s “Junk Mail”
Directory of Major Mailers
SRDS
InsideDirectMail.com
Web Sites
Buy from the Best
Criteria of a Code Owner Worth Watching
Direct Response
Print and/or Direct Mail if Possible
Company of size, doing a lot of testing
Active in Your Category or Comparable
Category
Longevity / Multiple Promotions Over
Time
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 24
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
At the end of three months, you give them a buck for every piece of junk mail
they threw in the basket. You’ve got three months’ worth of junk mail and
they’re excited.
Now, you can take it all home and you can sift it into piles and you can check
carefully to see that these five pieces are really from the same place and this one
came three times during the month – that probably means it’s a control.
You can now create your tabletop from which you are going to crack the code.
There’s a book called the “Directory of Major Mailers” which you can get, which
tells you who’s mailing how much to whom.
Then you can go Google them and you can find their website and you can find
their address and you can buy something from them.
Then you’ll be on their mailing list plus you’ll be on the mailing lists of
everybody they rent their mailing list to.
Bingo, you will soon have a big table full of stuff that you can begin to sift and
sort and use to crack the code.
There’s a reference called Standard Rate and Data Service – SRDS.com – it’s a
commercial marketplace of mailing lists.
It will tell you who’s got what size list; how many new names they get every 90
days; how many come from direct mail; how many come from these other
sources.
When you find somebody who is very analogous to you that you want to model,
there’s another thing you can do, you can act like you want to rent that list –
whether you do or not. You are then going to get a thing called a data card and
one of the things it’s going to tell you is who’s renting that list on a continuous
basis.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 25
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
You then can go to the Inside Direct Mail archives and you can order the
controls of everybody on that list. They make Xeroxes of them and send them to
you. You don’t even have to go find them.
Now, if we go find the Investor’s Business Daily master file of people who not
only subscribe to Investor’s Business Daily but buy stuff from it – their best list –
we see there’s eight companies that are mailing it all the time.
We then go to Inside Direct Mail. We say, “Give me the current controls of those
eight companies” and, bingo, we’ve got them.
Now, we can search them for commonalities and we can crack the code.
Not only can we crack the code in general in that category, we’ve cracked the
code of who’s selling to that list.
If we’re going to mail to that list or advertise in that publication, we can – if we’ll
do a little work – have a code list just for it.
The same ad really shouldn’t run in four different publications
without some changes because there may be different code.
We can go to their websites. We can sign up for their email lists, we can go to
their webinars etc.
Of course, buy from the best; spend
a little money.
Creating Your Templates
Now, how do we turn all these into
a template?
First of all, you can decide
arbitrarily on the big architecture
you wish to use for copy.
How to Create Your Writing Templates
Decide on the “Big” Architecture
Put Selected Code Items in Order Within
the Big Architecture
Create Headlines and Subheads for each
code item tied to the particular
proposition you are selling
= OUTLINE
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 26
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
I happen to like “Problem - Agitate Problem – Invalidate Option – Solve” more
than I like anything else.
If you examine my work and you want to look for an architecture code, you’ll
find that pretty quick.
You’ll see I use it over and over and over; I just hang different clothes on it but
the architecture is the same.
Some people like the oldest sales formula: “Attention – Interest – Desire –
Action”.
So you can decide on an architecture.
Then you can take your code items from your code list and put them in order in
the architecture:
 What do you think should go first under Attention?
 What do you think should go first under Interest?
 What do you think should go first under Desire?
You can line them up. Then you can create your headlines and your subheads
from that and bingo, guess what you got, you got an outline.
You can also take anybody’s control. So, if you go through the whole exercise I
just did in the investment newsletter category and we identify now that list
which I gave you as a sample, the code list, if we can find a control that has all
those items in it, we can convert it to a template.
For example, this is the first page of that control that I showed you for the
801(k) plans (page 13).
I have converted it into a template to write a sales letter from it.
The template is there’s a pre-headline, which uses number one on my code list.
There’s a main headline which uses item 12 and 13 on my code list.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 27
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
There’s a secondary headline
which uses number nine on my
code list.
There’s an intro paragraph above
the salutation which uses number
two plus four on my code list;
opening paragraph uses five, nine
and twelve and the paragraphs in
the latter part of the first page use
number three.
I now have a template.
If I am going to write to these same buyers, I can write a sales letter to fit that
template.
All I need is my template and my code list.
So I’m going to write a pre-headline and I’m going to use number one. What’s
number one? Alarm. Okay, I need an alarm pre-headline for what I’m selling.
Now, we could go through the whole letter, page by page by page and convert
every page into a template just like I did here.
We could then go back and write a sales letter for our investment newsletter
built on that same successful template that has already come from an enormous
amount of testing, that we either aren’t able to do or don’t want to do.
Now, if this sounds arduous to you, if you’re going to generate a lot of copy and
if you’re going to generate a lot of promotions, it’s a lot less arduous than
starting from scratch every time you’re going to write copy.
Example Template
#1: Pre-headline
#12 + #13 Main Headline
#9 Secondary Headline
#2 + #4 Intro Paragraph Above Salutation
#5 + #9 + #12 Opening Paragraphs
#3 Paragraphs in Latter Part of First Page
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 28
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
Yes, this takes a little time to build but, once it’s built, you get to use it over and
over and over again because it doesn’t wear out.
Freelance copywriters, those of us who make our living pretty much writing
copy, tend – at least over time, if not initially – to get pretty narrow in the
categories in which we will write and we turn down a lot of other things.
The reason for that is we’ve got templates in that category where we have
already done a lot of work.
We’ve got Copy Banks – which I’m going to talk about in a second – in that
category.
If you want me to do skin care, I’ve got templates for that; I’ve already got Copy
Banks for that. I’ve already done all this for that. I just get to use it again and
again and again.
The pro copywriters, especially the high-income ones, tend to wind up only
writing copy in three or four different categories of products or services
because they want to reuse this stuff over and
over and over again.
The real meticulous ones may check it like I just
did in Chris’ case but the check was pretty quick
because I already had templates and swipe files
tied to my code. I’ve got my code and I’ve got a
Copy Bank.
How to Create a Copy Bank
The last piece of this puzzle is a Copy Bank. The
Copy Bank is, you’ve decided on your categories, you’ve built templates, write
copy hunks from your code for every one of those categories, lots of them.
Just anytime you’ve got a few spare minutes, write some copy. You don’t have to
need it this minute so get 500 opening paragraphs that use “alarm” done that
If you’re going to
generate a lot of
copy, it’s a lot less
arduous than
starting from
scratch every time.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 29
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
have some relationship to your business and have them in a box or a computer
file. Now, you got a Copy Bank.
You definitely want a vocabulary list.
A good Copy Bank has a pre-done
vocabulary list.
It’s like your own thesaurus.
What are the words you should use to describe
Armageddon? What are the words you should
use to describe opportunity? What are the
words…?
I build that for clients, the last one I built billed at a million dollars.
So this is worth you taking the time to do if you’re going to do a lot of copy in
your category – or if you’re going to have copywriters work for you because if
you can hand them a Copy Bank to work from, you make everybody’s life a lot
easier and a lot more efficient.
You organize your swipe material by each one of these categories and ideally,
you’ve got one item on each card, on a piece of paper or in a computer file so you
can print out just the one thing that you want.
You want your Copy Bank ultimately to work like a Chinese menu.
So it is now time to write. I pick or build my template and then I pick one item
from column A and one item from column B and one item from column C and I
stick them in the template and then I smooth out the edges.
That at least gets you to rough draft if not to finished draft.
I’ve asked a bunch of pro copywriters, freelancers, how many drafts they do and
many of them say four, five, six, seven, eight drafts. I never do more than two,
never.
You want your Copy
Bank ultimately to
work like a Chinese
menu.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 30 --

HOW TO CREATE IRRESISTIBLE SALES MESSAGES FOR ANY BUSINESS PAGE 30
© 2014 GKIC Module 1
I do a first draft and a final draft. That’s what I do.
The first draft is what I just described to you in most of the cases. It’s Chinese
menued into a template, smooth out the edges.
The final draft is finishing it off, making it sound better; I got an inspiration for
an idea I can fit in here.
First draft, final draft, out the door; that’s the power of a great Copy Bank.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 30 --
